name: Darrowmere Lake
region: Lordaeron lake surrounding Caer Darrow and separating the Barov surface from Scholomance's island stronghold.

sensoryProfile.sight: Low white haze over green-black water, one shoved-free boat, a green ward shimmer over the approach, and Caer Darrow resolving out of mist in hard pieces.
sensoryProfile.sound: Wrapped oarlocks, small black sounds of water on the hull, and a monitored quiet that feels like listening.
sensoryProfile.smell: Wet stone, mineral cold, lake damp, and the first sterile edge of preserving work when the island draws close.
sensoryProfile.touch: Ward pressure at the back of the teeth, slick mortar under the hands, and mud gripping the keel on the return shore.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: A watched threshold. The lake turns approach, breach, and escape into one continuous test.

establishedFacts.fact: The crossing to Caer Darrow is monitored by an outer ward line that flags magical entrants as they pass.
Mist and water work with the ward architecture to isolate the island and erase easy extraction behind the boat.
The lake crossing is externally observable enough that ferry watch can confirm passage to Caer Darrow without knowing what happens inside the island keep.
The outer ward over Darrowmere can be muted by hugging the east wall rather than taking the public landing straight across.
A covert crossing over Darrowmere remains viable by boat if noise discipline and wall pressure are managed together.

eventsHere.event: Jandice rows Jaina across the lake, making the threshold from estate courtesy into Scholomance control physically unavoidable.
Relay watchers confirm Jaina crossed Darrowmere toward Caer Darrow and did not return the same night.
Sylvanas and Cyndia cross at the east wall, using the lake itself as cover for a side-window breach into Caer Darrow.
Jaina, Sylvanas, and Cyndia make the return crossing in silence before the southbound relay turns the lake behind them into closed ground again.
